     The Numbers Business Report

A Beautiful Planet (2016)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $15,650,615Details
International Box Office $9,851,573
Worldwide Box Office $25,502,188
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $388,534
Total $388,534
Further financial details...

Synopsis

A portrait of Earth from space, providing a unique perspective and increased understanding of our planet and galaxy as never seen before. The film features footage of our magnificent blue planet — and the effects humanity has had on it over time — captured by the astronauts aboard the International Space Station. From space, Earth blazes at night with the electric intensity of human expansion — a direct visualization of our changing world. But it is within our power to protect the planet. As we continue to explore and gain knowledge of our galaxy, we also develop a deeper connection to the place we all call home.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$223,983 (1.4% of total gross)
Legs:69.87 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:61.4% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Theater counts:137 opening theaters/155 max. theaters, 9.2 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $20,463,398

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: April 29th, 2016 (IMAX) by Walt Disney
International Releases: May 27th, 2016 (Wide) (United Kingdom)
August 26th, 2016 (Limited) (Russia (CIS))
September 2nd, 2016 (IMAX), released as A Beautiful Planet 3d (Australia)
September 23rd, 2016 (Wide), released as Beautiful Planet, A (New Zealand)
March 9th, 2026 (IMAX) (Australia)
Video Release: December 11th, 2018 by Mill Creek Entertainment
MPA Rating: G
(Rating bulletin 2417 (Cert #50350), 3/23/2016)
Running Time: 45 minutes
Cast, crew, or production detail: Short Film, Voiceover/Narration
Film format: IMAX: DMR
Geographical setting: Space Station
Social setting: Space Program
Subgenre: Science Documentary
Source:Based on Real Life Events
Genre:Documentary
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Factual
Production/Financing Companies: Walt Disney Pictures, IMAX Corporation, National Aeronautics and Space Administration
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English

Synopsis

A portrait of Earth from space, providing a unique perspective and increased understanding of our planet and galaxy as never seen before. The film features footage of our magnificent blue planet — and the effects humanity has had on it over time — captured by the astronauts aboard the International Space Station. From space, Earth blazes at night with the electric intensity of human expansion — a direct visualization of our changing world. But it is within our power to protect the planet. As we continue to explore and gain knowledge of our galaxy, we also develop a deeper connection to the place we all call home.
